scientific_integrity.detail: The evaluated outputs remained hypothesis- and protocol-design oriented and did not fabricate empirical results.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package stays within research planning scope and does not cross into clinical or diagnostic decision-making.

methodological_ground.detail: Methods, controls, and validation logic are framed as proposals to be tested rather than established facts.

code_usability.detail: The package focuses on design logic and narrative outputs rather than executable scientific code.

functional_suitability.note: The package gives a clear two-stage design path from topic framing to experiment outline drafting.

reliability.note: Input validation and exact-count rules are documented, though the output quality still depends on user-supplied topic specificity.

performance_context.note: The two-step prompt design keeps token usage minimal for a complete biomedical research outline.

agent_usability.note: The workflow is short, cold-start friendly, and easy to apply in a single pass.

human_usability.note: The When-to-Use guidance and two-step workflow make the skill straightforward for biomedical researchers to discover and apply.

security.note: The skill stays within planning scope and explicitly forbids fabrication.

maintainability.note: The package is compact and reference-driven, with low maintenance overhead.

agent_specific.note: Triggering, step order, and exact-count requirements are clear; richer exemplars would improve edge-case consistency.

inputs.label: Mechanistic oncology study
inputs.note: The output stayed within planning scope and preserved a logical progression from question to validation.

assertions.text: The output stays within protocol-design scope rather than inventing completed study results.
assertions.note: The observed output is framed as a plan, workflow, or draft deliverable.

assertions.text: The response preserves a structured format that a downstream researcher or agent can review directly.
assertions.note: The output shape remains checklist-driven and audit-friendly.

assertions.text: The workflow moves from question framing toward validation steps instead of remaining purely descriptive.
assertions.note: The generated plan preserves progressive study logic.

assertions.text: Edge cases request missing design anchors before committing to a high-confidence protocol.
assertions.note: The prompt stays bounded by concrete inputs.

key_strengths: The skill keeps research planning outputs in a deterministic two-step structure.
Progression rules make the outline move from screening to validation instead of drifting into generic brainstorming.
The package is small, easy to audit, and directly reusable for biomedical topic framing.

recommendations.title: Add one negative example for over-broad topics
recommendations.problem: Edge prompts can still receive a structurally correct but under-constrained outline.
recommendations.root_cause: The package explains what good progression looks like but not what to refuse or narrow.
recommendations.fix: Add one canonical refusal-or-narrowing example for vague disease-only or pathway-only requests.
